TSLA: Cathie Wood Dumps Millions in Tesla and Meta Shares. Cathie Wood's ARK Invest has significantly reduced its holdings in Tesla and Meta, selling shares worth millions from both companies. This strategic shift moves funds away from large-cap tech toward mid-cap growth stocks like Trade Desk, indicating a rotation into sectors perceived to have better growth prospects. ARK also adjusted positions in Iridium Communications and other smaller companies, reflecting an evolving investment strategy.

LiveOne Announces Tesla Subscription Pricing. LiveOne, formerly Slacker Radio, has introduced Tesla-exclusive subscription pricing following the lapse of a free premium agreement. Two tiers are offered: LiveOne Plus ($3.99/month or $34.99/year) provides an ad-free experience with unlimited skips and premium stations, while LiveOne Premium ($9.99/month or $99.99/year) adds on-demand listening, queuing, and download capabilities, though these are not currently supported in Tesla vehicles. Owners not subscribing will experience ads and limited skips after December 1st.

Toyota Again Tops Consumer Reports Annual Auto Reliability Survey. Toyota has reclaimed the top spot in Consumer Reports' Annual Auto Reliability Survey, surpassing Subaru, with Lexus coming in third. Asian brands continue their dominance in the top 10, while Tesla significantly improved its ranking. The survey highlights hybrids as very reliable, while plug-in hybrids and EVs still face significant "growing pains" with 80% more problems than traditional internal combustion engine vehicles.

Elon Musk Says His 'Running Robot' Will 'Actually Eliminate Poverty' As He Shares Video Of Tesla Optimus Jogging. Elon Musk recently shared a video of Tesla's Optimus humanoid robot jogging in a lab, marking a new milestone for the project. Musk believes Optimus could significantly reshape the global economy and potentially eliminate poverty by reducing the need for human labor. Tesla anticipates starting mass production of Optimus soon, with an estimated price range of $20,000 to $30,000 per robot.

Tesla makes first appearance in Consumer Reports’ top 10 reliability rankings. Tesla has entered Consumer Reports' top 10 reliability rankings for the first time, rising to ninth place from 17th last year. This improvement is attributed to better build quality and a "standout" electric drive system, especially benefiting from its older car models which have seen continuous refinement. Despite ongoing issues with electrically controlled doors and an aging lineup, Tesla's enhanced reliability has also pushed it into the top 10 of Consumer Reports' comprehensive "automotive report card."

Apple, Tesla accused of profiting from abuses, environment destruction. Apple Inc. and Tesla Inc. are facing separate lawsuits alleging "deceptive marketing" by portraying their products as ethically and sustainably manufactured. The lawsuits claim both companies profit from raw materials like cobalt and coltan sourced from the Democratic Republic of Congo, which are linked to child labor, forced labor, and severe human rights and environmental abuses. Both companies are accused of violating consumer protection laws, with Apple denying the claims and Tesla not issuing a response.
TGT: 3 No-Brainer Dividend Stocks to Buy Right Now. This article identifies three dividend stocks — Cardinal Health (CAH), Chevron (CVX), and Target (TGT) — that offer compelling long-term prospects for dividend investors. It highlights Cardinal Health's successful transformation and potential for aggressive dividend growth, Chevron's renewed focus on shareholder value, and Target's sustainable high yield and potential for future substantial dividend increases amid a turnaround. The author emphasizes considering dividend growth track record and safety, not just yield, when evaluating dividend investments.

Target reportedly piloting overnight delivery. Target Corp. is piloting a new overnight delivery service in Cleveland, utilizing a dedicated sortation center and freelance delivery drivers to speed up online order fulfillment. This initiative comes as Target's e-commerce sales improve despite overall revenue declines, putting it in competition with ultrafast delivery efforts from Amazon and Walmart. The Cleveland pilot is part of Target's broader strategy to enhance its delivery capabilities, building on previous sortation center trials to streamline online order processing.

Shredded cheese sold at Walmart, Target and Aldi recalled. What to know. Over a million bags of shredded cheese, sold at major retailers including Walmart, Target, and Aldi, have been recalled due to potential metal fragments. Great Lakes Cheese Co. initiated the recall, which the FDA reclassified as Class 1, indicating potential temporary or reversible adverse health consequences. Consumers are advised to discard or return affected products for a refund, though the company states all recalled items have been removed from shelves.
SBUX: Starbucks Corporation (NASDAQ:SBUX) Declares $0.62 Quarterly Dividend. Starbucks Corporation (NASDAQ:SBUX) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.62 per share, payable on February 27, 2026, to shareholders of record on February 13, 2026. This marks the 15th consecutive year the company has increased its dividend, offering an annualized yield of 2.9%. While the current payout ratio is high at 82.9%, analysts anticipate an improved payout ratio of 67.8% next year based on projected EPS of $3.66.
DIS: Annandale Capital LLC Trims Stock Holdings in The Walt Disney Company $DIS. Annandale Capital LLC significantly reduced its stake in The Walt Disney Company by 96.5% during the second quarter, now holding only 1,739 shares worth $216,000. While Annandale Capital sold off shares, other institutional investors like Vanguard Group Inc., Geode Capital Management LLC, Kingstone Capital Partners Texas LLC, Norges Bank, and Jennison Associates LLC either increased their positions or initiated new ones in the entertainment giant. The article also provides a financial overview of Disney, including its market cap, PE ratio, recent earnings, and analyst ratings.

American Airlines partners with the Gary Sinise Foundation to host 20th annual Snowball Express. American Airlines has partnered with the Gary Sinise Foundation for the 20th annual Snowball Express, an event honoring families of fallen U.S. military service members and first responders. The airline will donate 16 charter aircraft and millions of AAdvantage miles to bring over 2,600 family members to Walt Disney World Resort for an all-expenses-paid experience. This initiative underscores American Airlines' ongoing commitment to supporting these families and ensuring their loved ones' sacrifices are remembered.

The Walt Disney Company (DIS): A Bull Case Theory. This article summarizes a bullish thesis on The Walt Disney Company (DIS) by LongYield, highlighting its significant progress in profitability and cash generation in FY-2025, with adjusted EPS growth of 19% and increased capital returns. The company's strategic refocusing across its entertainment, sports, and experiences segments, including streaming profitability and strong park performance, positions it for continued momentum despite industry challenges.

Netflix Pulls Further Ahead While Disney Struggles to Stabilize Legacy Media. Netflix continues to outperform Disney, demonstrating stronger growth, higher operating margins, and superior free cash flow generation, largely due to its focused streaming model. While Disney's streaming segment shows growth, its overall financial performance is weighed down by struggling legacy media assets. The two companies' contrasting business models present different investment profiles and future outlooks.
NKE: UK Watchdog Slams Nike For Misleading 'Sustainable' Claim In Online Ad. The UK's Advertising Standards Authority (ASA) has ruled against Nike for making a misleading "sustainable" claim in an online ad. The ad, promoting "Nike Tennis Polo Shirts – … Sustainable Materials," was found to lack a clear definition of sustainability and evidence of no negative environmental impact over the products' full life cycle. This decision highlights increasing scrutiny on green marketing in the fashion industry.
